trigger: Both preregistered full-precision candidates failed the unchanged development eligibility threshold; coder-1p5b scored 2/4 and general-1p7b scored 1/4.
outcome_visibility: Development-only eligibility outputs are visible. No P1, P2, or P3 calibration, validation, or held-out outcome exists.

added_candidate.selection_threshold: unchanged: at least 3 of 4 exact development-only tasks and no runtime boundary violation

unchanged_fields: zero external inference and zero service spend

budget_impact.storage_action: After exact snapshot manifests and failed outputs are retained, remove the two rejected full-precision caches through the Hugging Face cache manager before downloading the amendment candidate.

rationale: The original gate was designed to reject weak runtimes before empirical preregistration. Testing one stronger, quantized, local-only model is an eligibility repair, not an outcome-driven empirical arm addition. The failed candidates remain negative knowledge.
stop_rule: If general-4b-mlx4 fails the unchanged threshold or cannot run inside the amended budgets, record a P1/P2 runtime activation blocker and do not add another candidate in this M2 cycle.
non_claims: The amendment does not erase or reinterpret the two failed candidates.
Quantization and parameter count do not establish quality or safety.
Eligibility remains development-only and cannot promote a chapter claim.
No held-out program outcome informed this amendment.
